top_jobs: using a music streaming service without restrictions
Find alternative ways to discover new music

unmet_needs.job_to_be_done: see play count for all songs

discovery_breaks_focus.text: How many others are listening to this song?. The ability to see how many other people in the world are *currently* listening to the same song as you

Song Tagging. We need an option to attach tags to a song (electronic, chill, soulful, etc.), as well as an option to sort based on these tags to create dynamic playlists. This would essentially be a manual version of the dynamic playlist option that is already on the app. This would help those of us with many playlists to keep track of songs and not worry about forgetting old playlists

[Mobile] See top 10 songs of artist like on desktop. I don't know why it isn't done on mobile also ?? but when I'm discovering new artists, I feel limited by the 5 tracks shown to me by spotify when I'm actually trying to get a glimpse of the artist's discography I would like the same as desktop, a "show-more button" to reveal more of the artist's work

discovery_buried_in_ui.text: Music comments. Now I hate to use another music platform as an example, but Soundcloud has a feature that allows you to see comments on a song. Why do want this on Spotify? In my personal opinion I think that comments are something that is just nice to see because I like seeing what other peoples thoughts are on a song. Allowing comments would allow a user to see the general public reaction to a song. Example: I like this song, I'm debating whether or not to show it to someone. I check the comments to see what other people have to say about it, if the comments are positive I'm more to likely to listen to the song and share it to others Cons: Negative feedback and bad spam on certain artists.  fix: block spam, filter certain words, have a message before a user comments saying that you will be banned from commenting if the comment is harsh towards the artist You could even make a feature that allows the user to turn it on and off if you want to see the comments or not. Like a hide comments button

Music Videos to always play option. I'm loving the music videos whilst using the desktop app but it's annoying that once that song is over the next song just plays normally even if it has a video.  I think there should be a "always play video when available" option (at least within the desktop app)

Add/remove a song from your liked songs with the press of one button! (heart icon). Please, can we go back to the user’s having the ability to add/remove a song from your liked songs with the press of one button? It’s down right frustrating that we have to now press three different buttons to do this one simple and critical task. The “heart” symbol on the screen in the bottom corner of the song playing was perfect. No idea why this changed, or how on earth I’m the only user to be inconvenienced to the point of taking the time to write this post lol

filter_bubble_overpersonalization.text: TURN OFF PERSONALISED PLAYLISTS. Recently, Spotify started customizing its own playlists (ie Spotify curated) based on what I like, but I go to these playlists (decades, dinner music playlists, etc.) to listen to something I don't usually listen to . Spotify, please stop customizing your own playlists to me and just play its standard curated playlist like it used to. PLEASE +VOTE if this issue is affecting you

Fix the awful shuffle. When I think of the word shuffle I think I will listen to every song in my playlist before I hear the same one again. Not for Spotify, they think it means play whatever songs they want. Even if you skip it everytime it still plays before a song I haven’t heard in a year. Give us a shuffle that plays every song before it repeats. Switch it up, I don’t want to hear the same 10 songs to start my shuffle everytime I reopen the app and start listening again

Add a "not interested button". Hello, Spotify keeps shuffling my true crime podcasts to one's I don't like. There is no way to stop this from happening and I can not remove the specific podcast from queue. It comes back. There should be an option, such as a not interested button, to stop a particular podcast or song/artists from playing. Please add something that will make these podcasts not come up as you ask them not to. Thank you,

no_control_over_algorithm.text: Option to remove DJ from Your Library. I recently tried the new DJ feature and, afterwards, the DJ playlist icon appeared in my Library. I did not pin this feature. I also cannot remove said feature. Why does a one-time use turn into permanent residence?

Smart Shuffle only playing recommended songs. We've received reports that the Smart Shuffle feature only plays recommended tracks. No liked songs or songs from the user's playlists are playing/visible in the queue

Block specific podcasts from auto-playing or appearing in recommendations. I rarely listen to podcasts, but tonight I decided to put on a sleep podcast since I was having trouble falling asleep. Unfortunately, after the sleep podcast ended Spotify started auto-playing a popular podcast that included an extremely graphic, violent and sexual rant. I woke up having a nightmare as a result of Spotify playing this disgusting content that could have been prevented.Turning off explicit content wouldn't have helped because the podcast wasn't even flagged as explicit in the first place, and besides I'm an adult and don't want to exclude good music just because it has an F bomb or two. I already reported the podcast, but there's no way to stop that specific podcast from auto-playing or showing up on my home page again which is disappointing. Heck, there's not even a way to mark it as "not interested" which would have been at least somewhat helpful

other.text: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs. The platform is increasingly flooded with AI-generated songs (especially the Release Radar), making it harder for users to discover authentic, human-created music. To improve the listening experience, Spotify should introduce a clear label for AI-generated songs and provide an option to filter them out entirely
Bring back Playlist radio. Radio is only available for artists and albums but not for playlists

Add Spotify Chart for Bangladesh. SPOTIFY CHARTS IN BANGLADESH Spotify was launched in Bangladesh since February 23, 2021 making music accessible to Bangladeshi locals, however due to absence of Bangladesh charts, it is impossible to see how local artists as well as international artists are performing in the region. Spotify has been in popular demand in Bangladesh over the years and the number of users has increased significantly. In view of general public interest as well as of BTS fans who want to support their favorite artists by streaming their music, we would like the introduction of regional Spotify charts in Bangladesh to see the artists popularity and demand in the region. It would prove helpful to Bangladeshi artists for them to see their demand in their hometown, and Bangladeshi fans to see how their favorite international musicians are doing in their own region. So We humbly ask for transparent Spotify charts in Bangladesh that reflect listeners’ real streams and support local artists. It’s time for accurate rankings, fair data and equal visibility for the Bangladeshi music industry. We hope this idea finds you in great health and is given a thought by Spotify Team. Thank You!

poor_new_release_surfacing.text: Top 5 Genre break down every year for wrapped. Spotify wrapped 2020(or earlier)-2023 had a top 5 genre break down, 2024 did not. I consider this to be one of the main parts of wrapped (I can see my top artists and songs through other apps and sites) so not having the genre break down is a huge step backwards

AI block to protect artists. I know we have all talked about the war on AI music. However, my biggest issue comes from the fact that when I listen to "Discover Weekly", "Release Radar", or a specific bands "radio" channel, and Spotify has fake AI songs submitted under the REAL bands name. If I 'block' it, then my algorithm thinks I no longer like that artist and it robs the actual band and the metrics show that the artists are losing support! For example, as you can se from the screenshots, I have been suggested songs by BulletBoys, Lynch Mob, and Britny Fox but they are AI songs. I like the artists, but I don't want these songs. How do I block this without it looking like I no longer like the bands? Obviously, I'm not a fan of the AI generated bands that pop up and force me to leave Spotify, go online to verify if they are real, then come back and block them. I believe we should have the ability to block this as well, but at least this doesn't impact real artists. This has GOT to be a format which protects the artists, or the artists will no longer use it, in which case, we won't either

Discover Weekly playlist is blank or missing entirely. We're seeing reports from users whose Discover Weekly playlist is blank, empty or just missing entirely. Attempting to open the playlist results in a "This content is no longer available" error

repetition_fatigue.text: bring enhance back. Bring Enhance back, i can appreciate smart shuffle but its honestly so much worse

Get rid of the garbage AI DJ that plays the same songs over and over under the same prompts. The AI DJ sucks lol why does it play the same five songs under the same introduction multiple times per day? Why would it automatically replay a song that I queued right after that song finished? This is such a garbage feature for your ever rising cost of a subscription

Remove an entire genre from my taste profile. I would like to be able to remove the entire genre of children’s music from my taste profile/algorithm. I nanny and play music for the kid, and now it’s influencing my taste profile. I got recommended a playlist of Blippi and other garbage kids music today. I have tried individually excluding songs from my taste profile, but it’s very labor-intensive and unrealistic

stale_recommendations.text: Issues with using "Search" - keyboard does not appear automatically. We received several reports from users about "Search" not working as expected on Android: the keyboard does not appear automatically when tapping on the magnifying glass icon

Button to disable podcasts, video and audio book suggestions. Please add a button or toggle that lets users disable podcast, video, and audiobook recommendations on the home screen. Spotify is, for many of us, primarily a music app. When the start page is filled with podcast headlines, videos, and audiobook suggestions, it starts to feel more like a social media feed than a place to enjoy music. I would really appreciate more user control over the home screen — for example, the ability to choose whether I want to see music only, or also podcasts, videos, and audiobooks. A calmer, more customizable start page would make Spotify much more enjoyable for users who mainly come for music

[All Devices] Bring back queue refreshing after selecting a song. Hello! I think it's a good idea to bring back the feature that when you select a new song the queue would automatically refresh, that was pretty useful! I will leave two videos at the end of this post to show how it was before and how it is now. At first, I thought it was just a bug, so I posted about this on Spotify community before and I was guided by a Moderator to try using the Ideas Board to bring the "feature" back. You can access the post through this link . I can state four reasons for that to come back: First, the algorithm related to make a consistent and random queue, loses it's sense since the queue will not refresh after selecting one song that would generate a "fitting" queue acordlying; Second, that is a major flaw in usability; Third, Spotify app on Windows (I don't know about the other OSs) does not have the an oficial option to turn off permanentally the Smart Shuffle and the easiest way to refresh the queue would just be selecting another song; Fourth, I have used Spotify in a lot of different computers with diferent specifications and the fact that we'll have to possibly pass through the Smart Shuffle as we manually refresh the queue, turning on and off the Shuffle is not good at all considering that the app may crash or have a poor performance in low budget computers. I don't know when this feature will be fully removed since it seems that just some accounts were affected by this new update. I really think this is a vital feature for the app. How it was before: https://youtu.be/cjRjX0kY6PA How it is now: https://youtu.be/xnBTTKg4_tE I appreciate any response. My best regards, 08/14/2025 Update: It seems that more and more people are missing the "feature" since a lot more people commented and liked both this idea and my post in Spotify's community. That makes me believe that more accounts were included in the test field, thus, more people are complaing about it. Edit 1: English improvement, minor corrections (english hard lol) and update added

trust_in_recs.text: Show play count for all songs in mobile app, not just the popular section.. Feb 2 2024 Update: Thanks to everyone who has voted! It appears that even though we hit the 180 vote requirement, the status has not changed...please continue to vote in hopes of catching the attention of the Spotify Dev Team! Jan 10 2024 Update: We did it, we hit 180! Thank you to everyone who voted. I’m still waiting for the status to change to reflect the next steps, but it might be automated. Please keep voting to move this up the list of features. Jan 6 2024 Update: Only need a few more votes to get to 180, the magic number to bring this to the developers attention. Please consider voting and sharing if you want this feature. Idea Summary: Show play count for every song in the mobile app. Instead of only showing play count for the top 5 “popular” songs, show it for all songs. This idea seems to have been brought up multiple times before. Please let’s make it happen! Benefits: 1. Being able to gauge an artists most popular songs, even if it’s no longer on the top 5 “popular” section. 2. Being able to sort a playlist by play count. 3. Gauging the most popular songs within an album

Free audiobook listening time for all Premium members. While I'm grateful for the move to bring audiobooks to Spotify, the exclusive benefit of free audiobooks for the "plan manager" in duo and family memberships seems unjust. It diminishes the premium experience for other members who pay the same amount as their friends and family but don't enjoy the full premium benefits. I acknowledge the cost-effectiveness of duo and family memberships, but the solution to a fairer listening experience could be to distribute the 15 hours among all members in your party. This way, duo members, for instance, would get 7.5 hours free—a reasonable compromise for the reduced cost. It ensures fairness to all users and gives users the option to customise their audiobook time based on their plan size and budget

Be able to see who likes your playlist. Hello!! I hope this idea will be loved by many. Whenever I see someone liking my playlist, I always would wonder who favorited it. It would be a fun and lovely addition to Spotify if there were a feature where you could see who likes your playlist

discovery_seeker_distrusts_algo.text: World Map as a New Way to Discover Music. Currently, Spotify search works great when I already know what I want to listen to. However, when I want to actively discover new music, discovery relies mostly on algorithms. A map-based search mode could work as an alternative way to explore music by country, region, and local genres. Users could select a place on a world map and see popular styles, playlists, and artists from that location. This would create a more conscious and cultural way to discover music, complementing existing recommendations

Half of the weekly playlists are filled with AI generated content. I swear, this thing is killing Spotify. I keep getting songs of the same "Artists" (*people writing on a prompt on a website) despite I downvoted their "songs" every single time I got one in my generated playlist. Release radar and Discovery weekly are important features for me, it's basically my only way to discover new music... I don't want AI cr*p in them. Why I don't have any option to filter fake music?!

Stop using AI - The product is a shell of itself. I am writing as a long‑time user who is deeply frustrated by the direction your product has taken. What was once an excellent music discovery and listening platform has been materially degraded by your increasing reliance on opaque, intrusive AI-driven algorithms. Your recommendation systems no longer serve the listener. Instead of enhancing discovery, they override user intent, repeatedly surface unwanted content, recycle the same tracks, and prioritize algorithmic guesswork over explicit user preferences. Even direct actions—likes, follows, skips, and manual playlist curation—are routinely ignored. The result is a product experience that feels manipulative rather than personalized. Spotify now behaves as though it knows better than the user, forcing a narrow, repetitive interpretation of taste instead of allowing organic exploration. The irony is hard to miss: the more “intelligent” the system claims to be, the less control the user actually has. This is not innovation. It is erosion of trust. If these changes are intentional, then Spotify has lost sight of why people chose the platform in the first place. If they are not intentional, then that points to a more serious lack of quality control and product accountability. Either way, the current experience is actively worse than it was years ago. I strongly encourage your product and leadership teams to reassess the overreach of your AI systems and restore meaningful user control before further damaging what was once a genuinely great service

focus_work_listener.text: Bring back Playlist radio. Radio is only available for artists and albums but not for playlists

Add Spotify Chart for Bangladesh. SPOTIFY CHARTS IN BANGLADESH Spotify was launched in Bangladesh since February 23, 2021 making music accessible to Bangladeshi locals, however due to absence of Bangladesh charts, it is impossible to see how local artists as well as international artists are performing in the region. Spotify has been in popular demand in Bangladesh over the years and the number of users has increased significantly. In view of general public interest as well as of BTS fans who want to support their favorite artists by streaming their music, we would like the introduction of regional Spotify charts in Bangladesh to see the artists popularity and demand in the region. It would prove helpful to Bangladeshi artists for them to see their demand in their hometown, and Bangladeshi fans to see how their favorite international musicians are doing in their own region. So We humbly ask for transparent Spotify charts in Bangladesh that reflect listeners’ real streams and support local artists. It’s time for accurate rankings, fair data and equal visibility for the Bangladeshi music industry. We hope this idea finds you in great health and is given a thought by Spotify Team. Thank You!

Spotify Wrapped - Access Previous Years' Wrapped Stories & Stats. I really wish Spotify can enable users to access all the previous years' Spotify Wrapped stories and statistics (since joining the app), to see information including minutes listened and the top 5 artsists and songs. Currently, we are only able to search for our previous years' top songs, but I think it would honestly be amazing to look back at stats of the Wrapped for the new year compared to previous years, would be a lovely feature and track record for personal memories and to share them with friends. I hope Spotify can seriously consider adding this

genre_explorer_filter_bubble.text: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs. The platform is increasingly flooded with AI-generated songs (especially the Release Radar), making it harder for users to discover authentic, human-created music. To improve the listening experience, Spotify should introduce a clear label for AI-generated songs and provide an option to filter them out entirely

Being able to gauge an artists most popular songs, even if it’s no longer on the top 5 “popular” section. 2. Being able to sort a playlist by play count. 3. TURN OFF PERSONALISED PLAYLISTS. Recently, Spotify started customizing its own playlists (ie Spotify curated) based on what I like, but I go to these playlists (decades, dinner music playlists, etc.) to listen to something I don't usually listen to . Spotify, please stop customizing your own playlists to me and just play its standard curated playlist like it used to. PLEASE +VOTE if this issue is affecting you

mood_context_listener.text: Music comments. Now I hate to use another music platform as an example, but Soundcloud has a feature that allows you to see comments on a song. Why do want this on Spotify? In my personal opinion I think that comments are something that is just nice to see because I like seeing what other peoples thoughts are on a song. Allowing comments would allow a user to see the general public reaction to a song. Example: I like this song, I'm debating whether or not to show it to someone. I check the comments to see what other people have to say about it, if the comments are positive I'm more to likely to listen to the song and share it to others Cons: Negative feedback and bad spam on certain artists.  fix: block spam, filter certain words, have a message before a user comments saying that you will be banned from commenting if the comment is harsh towards the artist You could even make a feature that allows the user to turn it on and off if you want to see the comments or not. Like a hide comments button

Music Haptics iOS 18 compatibility. Apple had a new software update(iOS 18) came out with a cool feature(Music Haptics) for deaf people(I am not), to be able to listen to music by the vibrations they feel through the phone. I prefer to use Spotify over Apple Music, it’d be cool if this was integrated so I could use this feature myself

Serbia - TOP 50 Charts. Spotify was launched in Serbia more than 3 years ago and there is still no Top 50 Charts for our country even though there are many people using your service. We hope that you will launch charts soon and that you will show us that users in Serbia are not less important to you than users from other countries

other.text: How many times have you listened to a certain song?. It would be nice if you are listening to a song, that you can see how many times you have listened to/played this song

DISLIKE BUTTON. If i hate a song, then I don't want to hear it ever again. All of us run into this on an hourly basis. Every single day. Why in the world cant we dislike (or thumb down) a song to remove it from existence? Why do you torture us by playing songs we don't want to hear? There is a "hide" option (only on mobile), and its playlist specific. The desktop version doesn't even have this somewhat-useless feature. So I vote for a universal dislike button. No playlist limitations. No desktop vs mobile limitations. No buttons hidden in submenus. Just a simple, easily accessible button that can eliminate our headaches once and for all

[Your Library] Keep lyrics up at all times. I like having the lyrics accessible, and having to swipe up and click lyrics is a pain. Instead of the album cover, it would be awesome to have the option to have the lyrics there instead!

workout_tempo_listener.text: Share My Listening To The "Friend Activity" Bar On Desktop When Listening On Mobile. I've noticed that when I am listening to music on mobile (this is primarily where I listen), it does not populate in the "Friend Activity" bar on desktop. This is something that existed for years that was just suddenly taken away. I cannot understand why this would be removed. If someone doesn't want their activity shared, they've always had the option to turn off sharing. I want people to be able to see what I am listening to all the time, not just when I am listening on desktop. BRING IT BACK!

Please fix Spotify Shuffle.. I've been wanting to talk about this for longest, since this has kind of been a problem for me within the past few years of me using Spotify. This may not exactly be an idea, but I just really want to talk about it. For context, I mostly use Spotify on my PS5 when I'm gaming and I think it's really great to use as it is free to use on there and doesn't require a subscription like Apple Music does, and I hope it stays that way. My main issue is with the shuffle option and how it does not actually shuffle the songs that are in queue. Mind you I have over 1,092 songs saved on my 'Liked Song' list, and it feels like I'm constantly getting the exactly same songs or songs that are similar to them, which is strange because I thought that 'shuffle' was completely random, but apparently shuffle the feature might run off of some kind of statistic, like songs that you play the most or something like that, which heard on either here or, and it probably explains why I wouldn't hear a song from an artist for months on my list and IMO completely defeats the purpose of having shuffle if it doesn't actually shuffle. There would be many times when I would even get the same exact songs in one session, and I'm like “did I not just hear that song?”. Like yesterday and the day before, I got the exact same queue of shuffled songs, it's that bad. Please fix the other bugs that may be plaguing Spotify, such as after an advertisement finishing. It just completely freezes, and it doesn't play the next song or next advertisement, and I would have to completely exit Spotify and replay my playlist, and it may not just be Spotify itself or just the version of Spotify that's on PS5. Maybe it could be different; I'm not sure, but I really hope this gets fixed. Thank you for reading

Smart shuffle improvement. Hello, I have a problem with your smart shuffles. Your smart shuffles seems to rely heavily , maybe 80% on my old liked songs. I don't think it should be the case. I use smart shuffles to find songs that may be similar to my profile or what i like not to shuffle old songs  that i have removed form my library. Please can it be improved. Maybe it should be 80% new songs and 20% old songs. The reason i have removed old songs is that i don't really want to hear it no more. So. .. I thank you for hearing my idea
